Part / Model: Holybro Pixhawk 6C Standard Set (FC + PM06 + M9N)
Why / Justification: The autonomy brain — do not cheapen this. Bundle includes the PM06 power module (battery voltage + current sensing → enables low-battery failsafe and accurate mission planning) and the M9N GPS/compass (position hold
column9: waypoint missions). Buying these 3 separately costs more.
column10: STM32H743 480MHz / dual redundant temp-controlled IMUs (Bosch + InvenSense) / internal vibration isolation / ArduPilot + PX4. ~$165.99 FC-only; $189.99 set. holybro.com

Why / Justification: One board drives all 4 motors — simpler wiring and lighter than 4 single ESCs. DShot gives clean digital throttle; AM32 firmware exposes RPM telemetry to the FC. 50A per channel is ample headroom over the X2814's ~12-18A hover draw on a ~1.4kg quad.
Spec & Source Notes: 4-6S input / DShot300-600 / 30.5x30.5mm mount / F4 MCU. holybro.com (60A $79.99 / 65A $87.99 if you want more margin)

Why / Justification: Correctly sized for 11-inch props at 1.2-1.4kg. Your current D2830-850KV is too small / over-propped for 11-inch — it runs hot and inefficient. The X2814 is the right stator for this prop+weight: cooler running and more efficient = longer autonomous missions. Ships with prop adapter.
Spec & Source Notes: 28mm stator / N48UH arc magnets / Japanese NMB bearings / 108g ea / 50A-680W max / 3-4S / rec. APC11x5.5-11x8. globerc / banggood ~$30

Part / Model: Gemfan 1147 11x4.7 multirotor (2x CW + 2x CCW) + 1 spare pair
Why / Justification: Matched to the X2814 + 4S for efficient lift. Stiff carbon-nylon blades reduce vibration = cleaner IMU data = better autonomous position hold. Spares included because props are the #1 crash casualty.
Spec & Source Notes: 11-inch x 4.7 pitch / fits X2814 adapter. ~$3-4/pr depending on vendor

Part / Model: XT60 pigtail + 12 AWG silicone wire + heatshrink
Why / Justification: Main power path from pack to ESC. 12 AWG carries full-throttle current of all 4 motors without voltage sag (sag = brownouts mid-flight).
Spec & Source Notes: 12 AWG / XT60 male to match CNHL packs

Why / Justification: Runs MAVLink / DroneKit + your vision stack and provides the telemetry/command link (over its own Wi-Fi/4G) — which is why a separate SiK radio isn't required here. A CM4 or Pi 5 is a future upgrade but won't fit this budget and isn't needed.
Works as-is. First upgrade when budget grows: CNHL 4S 4000-5000mAh (~$42) for longer mission time on the heavier motors.
11-inch-class frame already sized for these motors/props.
Comes in the box — no separate purchase.
Each motor ships with its prop adapter/mount.
Grand total within the $400 sponsor budget
